title: BlackSwan Stress Test Response
description: Schema for responses produced by blackswan-core. Every engine response must validate against this schema.

version.description: Contract version. Must match the request version.

status.description: High-level result. 'no_failures' means all iterations completed with zero shatter points.

runtime_ms.description: Total wall-clock time for the simulation run in milliseconds.

iterations_completed.description: Number of simulation iterations actually completed (may be less than requested if cancelled).

total_failures.description: Total number of iterations in which at least one failure was detected.

failure_rate.description: Fraction of completed iterations that contained at least one failure.

unique_failure_types.description: Number of distinct failure_type values observed across all shatter points.

shatter_points.description: Ordered list of detected failures, most severe first.

id.description: Stable identifier for this shatter point within the response (e.g. sp_001).

line.description: 1-indexed source line where the failure manifests. Null when attribution could not be resolved (Phase 1 limitation).

column.description: 0-indexed column offset within the line. Null in Phase 1 (column attribution not yet implemented).

severity.description: critical = model produces incorrect results; warning = potential instability; info = advisory.

failure_type.description: Machine-readable failure category. Maps to detector class.

message.description: Human-readable explanation. Must state: what failed, the observed value, and the threshold that was violated.

causal_chain.description: Ordered list from root input to failure site. Empty in Phase 1 (full causal walk implemented in Phase 2).

fix_hint.description: Actionable suggestion for how to guard against this failure. Empty string in Phase 1.

scenario_card.description: Captures exactly how the simulation was run so results can be reproduced.

parameters_applied.description: Key-value map of all scenario parameters as actually applied (after any clamping/defaults).

reproducible.description: Must be true. If the engine cannot guarantee determinism, it must return an error instead.
